standard.notes: 'Aller au bureau' = to go to the office. 'Mon bureau' = my desk. 'Le bureau à domicile' = the home office. 'Un bureau ouvert' = an open-plan office. The same word means both the physical desk and the workplace (office). 'Travailler en bureau' = to work in an office.
examples: Je travaille dans un bureau au centre-ville.
I work in an office in the city centre.
Mon bureau est près de la fenêtre.
My desk is near the window.

standard.notes: Feminine: 'la collègue'. 'Un bon collègue' = a good colleague. 'Mes collègues de travail' = my work colleagues. In French workplaces, colleagues often use 'tu' with each other but 'vous' with managers. 'Déjeuner avec des collègues' = to have lunch with colleagues.
examples: Mon collègue m'aide avec le projet.
My colleague helps me with the project.
Je mange avec mes collègues à midi.
I eat with my colleagues at noon.

standard.notes: 'Un ordinateur portable' = a laptop. 'Allumer l'ordinateur' = to turn on the computer. 'Éteindre l'ordinateur' = to turn off the computer. The word was coined in French in 1955 — most other languages borrowed 'computer' from English, but French created their own word.
examples: Je travaille sur mon ordinateur toute la journée.
I work on my computer all day.
L'ordinateur est en panne.
The computer is broken.

standard.notes: 'Avoir une réunion' = to have a meeting. 'La réunion commence à neuf heures' = the meeting starts at 9. 'Une réunion en ligne' = an online meeting. France has a reputation for long meetings ('réunionite' — meeting sickness — is a French word for having too many meetings).
examples: J'ai une réunion à dix heures.
I have a meeting at ten o'clock.
La réunion dure deux heures.
The meeting lasts two hours.

standard.notes: 'Aller au travail' = to go to work. 'Chercher un travail' = to look for a job. 'Le travail est terminé' = the work is done. Plural: 'les travaux' = works / roadworks. 'Bon travail !' = Good work! 'Travail à temps plein' = full-time work. 'À mi-temps' = part-time.
examples: J'aime mon travail.
I like my job.
Le travail commence à huit heures et demie.
Work starts at 8:30.

advanced.notes: 'Aller au bureau' = to go to the office. 'Mon bureau' = my desk. 'Le bureau à domicile' = the home office. 'Un bureau ouvert' = an open-plan office. The same word means both the physical desk and the workplace (office). 'Travailler en bureau' = to work in an office.
examples: Je travaille dans un bureau au centre-ville.
I work in an office in the city centre.
Mon bureau est près de la fenêtre.
My desk is near the window.

advanced.notes: Feminine: 'la collègue'. 'Un bon collègue' = a good colleague. 'Mes collègues de travail' = my work colleagues. In French workplaces, colleagues often use 'tu' with each other but 'vous' with managers. 'Déjeuner avec des collègues' = to have lunch with colleagues.
examples: Mon collègue m'aide avec le projet.
My colleague helps me with the project.
Je mange avec mes collègues à midi.
I eat with my colleagues at noon.

advanced.notes: 'Un ordinateur portable' = a laptop. 'Allumer l'ordinateur' = to turn on the computer. 'Éteindre l'ordinateur' = to turn off the computer. The word was coined in French in 1955 — most other languages borrowed 'computer' from English, but French created their own word.
examples: Je travaille sur mon ordinateur toute la journée.
I work on my computer all day.
L'ordinateur est en panne.
The computer is broken.

advanced.notes: 'Avoir une réunion' = to have a meeting. 'La réunion commence à neuf heures' = the meeting starts at 9. 'Une réunion en ligne' = an online meeting. France has a reputation for long meetings ('réunionite' — meeting sickness — is a French word for having too many meetings).
examples: J'ai une réunion à dix heures.
I have a meeting at ten o'clock.
La réunion dure deux heures.
The meeting lasts two hours.

advanced.notes: 'Aller au travail' = to go to work. 'Chercher un travail' = to look for a job. 'Le travail est terminé' = the work is done. Plural: 'les travaux' = works / roadworks. 'Bon travail !' = Good work! 'Travail à temps plein' = full-time work. 'À mi-temps' = part-time.
examples: J'aime mon travail.
I like my job.
Le travail commence à huit heures et demie.
Work starts at 8:30.

advanced.notes: Feminine: 'la patronne'. 'Mon patron est strict' = My boss is strict. Informal but very common; the formal equivalent is 'le directeur' / 'la directrice' (director) or 'le responsable'. 'Patron' can also mean 'saint patron' (patron saint). 'Le patron du café' = the café owner.
examples: Je parle à mon patron de la réunion.
I'm talking to my boss about the meeting.
La patronne donne les instructions à l'équipe.
The boss gives instructions to the team.

advanced.notes: 'Chercher un emploi' = to look for a job. 'L'emploi du temps' = the timetable / schedule. 'Le marché de l'emploi' = the job market. More formal than 'le travail'. 'Un emploi à plein temps' = a full-time job. 'Un emploi précaire' = precarious employment.
examples: Il cherche un emploi dans le marketing.
He is looking for a job in marketing.
Mon emploi du temps est chargé cette semaine.
My schedule is busy this week.

dialogue: Bienvenue ! C'est ton premier jour. Je te montre le bureau.
Merci ! Et pour la réunion de ce matin — qu'est-ce que je dois apporter ?
Apporte ton ordinateur. Je lui donne le dossier avant la réunion.
Et les autres collègues — je leur envoie un email ?
Oui, tu leur envoies un message pour confirmer l'heure.
D'accord. Et le patron — je lui parle maintenant ?
Non, je lui parle d'abord. Il est dans son bureau.

questions.question: What should speaker B bring to the meeting?
What does speaker A give to the boss?

standard.summary: Speaker A is showing speaker B around on their first day at work. For the morning meeting, B should bring their computer. A will give the boss the file before the meeting. B should email the other colleagues to confirm the time. A will speak to the boss first before B does.

dialogue: Bonjour et bienvenue dans l'équipe ! Je t'explique le fonctionnement.
Merci ! Pour la réunion de cet après-midi — je lui envoie le rapport au patron avant ?
Oui, envoie-lui le rapport avant midi. Et les collègues — tu leur envoies l'ordre du jour aussi.
D'accord. Et si j'ai des questions sur l'ordinateur ?
Tu demandes à Marie — je lui ai déjà expliqué ton emploi du temps.
Super. Et le patron — il aime qu'on lui parle directement ou par email ?
Il préfère qu'on lui envoie un message d'abord. Il répond toujours.
Noté ! Je lui envoie un message maintenant pour me présenter.

questions.question: What should speaker B send to the boss before noon?
What should speaker B send to the colleagues?
Who has speaker A already told about B's schedule?
How does the boss prefer to be contacted?

advanced.summary: Speaker A welcomes B to the team and explains procedures. B should send the boss the report before noon and send the colleagues the agenda. A has already explained B's schedule to Marie (the IT/admin contact). The boss prefers to be messaged first rather than approached directly — and always replies. B decides to message the boss immediately to introduce themselves.

standard.title: Indirect Object Pronouns (lui, leur)
standard.explanation:
Indirect object pronouns replace a noun that follows **à** and refers to a person — they answer 'to whom?' or 'for whom?'.

**The two pronouns:**
| French | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **lui** | to him / to her |
| **leur** | to them |

**Position:** Before the verb (like direct object pronouns).

**Common verbs that use indirect objects (with à):**
- **parler à** (to speak to): *Je lui parle.* (I speak to him/her.)
- **donner à** (to give to): *Je lui donne le dossier.* (I give him/her the file.)
- **envoyer à** (to send to): *Tu leur envoies l'email.* (You send them the email.)
- **dire à** (to say to): *Il lui dit bonjour.* (He says hello to him/her.)
- **téléphoner à** (to phone): *Je lui téléphone.* (I phone him/her.)

**Examples:**
- Je parle **au patron**. → Je **lui** parle. (I speak to the boss.)
- Elle envoie un email **aux collègues**. → Elle **leur** envoie un email. (She sends them an email.)

**Note:** *Lui* = to him OR to her (no gender distinction!)

examples.french: Il lui parle avant la réunion.
examples.english: He speaks to him/her before the meeting.

drills.question: Replace 'au patron': Je parle au patron. → Je ___ parle.
drills.explanation: 'Au patron' = to the boss (singular). Use 'lui' for 'to him'. Je lui parle.

drills.question: Replace 'aux collègues': Tu envoies un email aux collègues. → Tu ___ envoies un email.
drills.explanation: 'Aux collègues' = to the colleagues (plural). Use 'leur' for 'to them'. Tu leur envoies.

drills.question: What does 'Je lui téléphone' mean?
drills.explanation: 'Lui' = to him / to her (both genders). Je lui téléphone = I phone him or her.

drills.question: Complete: Elle ___ dit bonjour. (She says hello to them.)
drills.explanation: 'Their' = à eux/elles (plural). 'Leur' replaces 'à + plural person'. Elle leur dit bonjour.

drills.question: Where does 'lui' / 'leur' go in the sentence?
options: At the end of the sentence
drills.explanation: Like all object pronouns, lui and leur go directly before the verb: Je lui donne (NOT Je donne lui).

advanced.title: Indirect Object Pronouns — lui, leur with All Tenses & Common Verbs
advanced.explanation:
**Indirect object pronouns replace 'à + person':**
| Person | Indirect pronoun |
|---|---|
| à moi | me (m' before vowel) |
| à toi | te (t' before vowel) |
| à lui / à elle | **lui** |
| à nous | nous |
| à vous | vous |
| à eux / à elles | **leur** |

**Position rules:**
| Tense | Example |
|---|---|
| Present | Je **lui** parle. |
| Passé composé | Je **lui** ai parlé. |
| Near future | Je vais **lui** parler. |
| Imperative (affirmative) | Parle-**lui** ! / Envoyez-**leur** ! |
| Imperative (negative) | Ne **lui** parle pas ! |

**Common verbs with indirect objects:**
- *parler à, donner à, dire à, envoyer à, téléphoner à*
- *écrire à, répondre à, demander à, montrer à, expliquer à*
- *plaire à* (to please): *Ça lui plaît.* (He/she likes it.)
- *manquer à* (to miss): *Tu me manques.* (I miss you — literally 'you are missing to me')

**Direct vs Indirect Object Pronouns:**
| Direct (le/la/les) | Indirect (lui/leur) |
|---|---|
| Je **le** vois. (I see him.) | Je **lui** parle. (I speak to him.) |
| Je **les** attends. (I wait for them.) | Je **leur** envoie. (I send to them.) |

**Key distinction:** Verbs like 'voir, aimer, appeler' take direct objects; 'parler, donner, envoyer' take indirect objects.

examples.french: Je lui ai envoyé le rapport avant midi.
examples.english: I sent him/her the report before noon.

examples.french: Je vais leur expliquer le projet.
examples.english: I am going to explain the project to them.

drills.question: Replace 'à la patronne': Je vais parler à la patronne. → Je vais ___ parler.
drills.explanation: 'À la patronne' = to the boss (singular, feminine). Use 'lui' (no gender distinction). Je vais lui parler.

drills.question: Which is correct for 'I sent them the email' (passé composé)?
drills.explanation: Passé composé: indirect object pronoun goes before the auxiliary (avoir). Je leur ai envoyé.

drills.question: Complete: Envoie-___ le rapport ! (Send him/her the report! — imperative)
drills.explanation: Affirmative imperative: verb + hyphen + pronoun. Envoie-lui le rapport !

drills.question: Which verb takes a direct object (le/la/les), NOT an indirect one?
drills.explanation: 'Voir' (to see) takes a direct object: Je le vois. 'Parler à, envoyer à, donner à' take indirect objects: Je lui parle.

drills.question: Complete: Ne ___ envoie pas l'email ! (Don't send them the email! — imperative negative)
drills.explanation: Negative imperative: ne + pronoun + verb + pas. Ne leur envoie pas l'email !

drills.question: What does 'Ça lui plaît' mean?
options: It pleases him/her / He/she likes it
drills.correctAnswer: It pleases him/her / He/she likes it
drills.explanation: 'Plaire à' = to be pleasing to. Ça lui plaît = it pleases him/her = he/she likes it.

drills.question: Complete: Je ___ ai expliqué l'emploi du temps. (I explained the schedule to him/her.)
drills.explanation: Passé composé with indirect pronoun before auxiliary: Je lui ai expliqué.

drills.question: What is the difference between 'Je le vois' and 'Je lui parle'?
options: 'Le' is direct object; 'lui' is indirect object (replaces à + person)
'Lui' is masculine only; 'le' is for all genders
'Le' is for things; 'lui' is only for people
drills.correctAnswer: 'Le' is direct object; 'lui' is indirect object (replaces à + person)
drills.explanation: Direct pronoun (le) replaces a direct object; indirect pronoun (lui) replaces 'à + person'.

standard.question: What does 'la réunion' mean?
standard.explanation: 'La réunion' = the meeting. 'J'ai une réunion à dix heures' = I have a meeting at 10.

standard.question: Replace 'au patron': Je parle au patron. → Je ___ parle.
standard.explanation: 'Au patron' = to the boss (singular). 'Lui' replaces 'à + singular person'. Je lui parle.

standard.question: What does 'l'ordinateur' mean?
standard.explanation: 'L'ordinateur' = the computer. A uniquely French word coined in 1955.

standard.question: Replace 'aux collègues': Tu envoies un email aux collègues. → Tu ___ envoies un email.
standard.explanation: 'Aux collègues' = to the colleagues (plural). 'Leur' replaces 'à + plural people'.

standard.question: What does 'le travail' mean?
standard.explanation: 'Le travail' = work/job. 'J'aime mon travail' = I like my job. Not to confuse with 'le voyage' (travel).

advanced.question: What does 'l'emploi du temps' mean?
advanced.explanation: 'L'emploi du temps' = timetable/schedule. 'Mon emploi du temps est chargé' = My schedule is busy.

advanced.question: Complete: Envoie-___ le rapport ! (Send him/her the report! — imperative)
advanced.explanation: Affirmative imperative with indirect pronoun: verb + hyphen + lui. Envoie-lui !

advanced.question: Which is correct for 'I sent them the email' (passé composé)?
advanced.explanation: Passé composé: indirect pronoun goes before the auxiliary avoir. Je leur ai envoyé.

advanced.question: What does 'le patron' mean?
advanced.explanation: 'Le patron' = the boss or owner (informal). Formal equivalent: 'le directeur'.

advanced.question: Complete: Ne ___ parle pas maintenant ! (Don't speak to him/her now! — imperative)
advanced.explanation: Negative imperative: ne + pronoun + verb + pas. Ne lui parle pas !

advanced.question: What does 'Ça lui plaît' mean?
advanced.explanation: 'Plaire à' = to be pleasing to. Ça lui plaît = it pleases him/her = he/she likes it.

standard.scenario: Describe your typical workday to a new colleague. Explain who you report to, mention the meetings you attend, and use indirect object pronouns (lui, leur) to describe how you communicate with your boss and colleagues.
keyPhrases: Je travaille dans un bureau… (I work in an office…)
J'ai une réunion à… (I have a meeting at…)
Je lui parle chaque matin. (I speak to him/her every morning.)
Je leur envoie un email. (I send them an email.)
Mon collègue m'aide avec… (My colleague helps me with…)
standard.modelAnswer: Je travaille dans un bureau au centre-ville. Ma journée commence à neuf heures. J'ai une réunion chaque matin — je leur envoie l'ordre du jour avant. Mon patron est très sympa — je lui parle tous les matins. Mes collègues sont sympas aussi — je déjeune avec eux. L'après-midi, je travaille sur mon ordinateur. J'aime mon travail !
standard.modelAnswerExplanation: The answer describes a typical workday, uses 'leur' (to send colleagues the agenda), 'lui' (to speak to the boss), includes meeting vocabulary and closes with a positive comment about the job.
standard.tip: Remember: 'lui' replaces 'à + one person', 'leur' replaces 'à + several people'. Always place these before the verb.

advanced.scenario: You are explaining your work life to a French contact. Describe your office, your boss, and your schedule. Use 'lui' and 'leur' in multiple tenses (present, passé composé, near future), and include 'l'emploi' and 'le patron'.
keyPhrases: Mon emploi du temps est… (My schedule is…)
Je lui ai envoyé le rapport ce matin. (I sent him/her the report this morning.)
Je vais leur expliquer le projet demain. (I'm going to explain the project to them tomorrow.)
Envoie-lui un message d'abord. (Send him/her a message first.)
Le patron préfère qu'on lui écrive. (The boss prefers that you write to him.)
advanced.modelAnswer: Mon emploi du temps est très chargé cette semaine. Ce matin, j'ai une réunion avec mon patron — je lui ai déjà envoyé le rapport hier soir. Mes collègues ne connaissent pas encore le projet — je vais leur expliquer demain. Mon patron préfère les emails — il dit toujours : 'Envoyez-moi un message d'abord !' Moi, j'aime bien mon travail même si les réunions sont longues. Je leur envoie souvent des emails pour tout coordonner.
advanced.modelAnswerExplanation: The answer uses 'lui' in passé composé (lui ai envoyé), 'leur' in near future (vais leur expliquer), affirmative imperative (envoyez-moi), and present tense — demonstrating all key tense positions.
